finding: Rule class was used as a manifold without separating parameters from operators.
action: Defined P_n, Theta_nk, and A_nk separately.

finding: Gaussian endpoint uniqueness was invoked but not proved.
risk: Endpoint theorem depended on an unstated uniqueness result.
action: Added orthogonal-polynomial and Vandermonde uniqueness proof.

finding: Alternation proof omitted uniqueness of the positive partial-fraction representation.
risk: Identical responses might appear to permit distinct rules.
action: Added a pole/residue uniqueness lemma and used it in the proof.

finding: Coefficient-box function class and convergence assumptions were implicit.
risk: The exact supremum theorem lacked a precise domain.
action: Defined H(C,rho), proved uniform convergence, and bounded the defect series.

finding: The exact mixed-risk theorem had no proof.
risk: Additive attainability was asserted rather than established.
action: Added compactness, sign alignment, and an attainment proof.

finding: Selector wording could imply global optimality over all quadrature rules.
action: Restricted exact optimality to the precomputed candidate set.

finding: Round-44 screening used sampled derivative inflation, not rigorous interval bounds.
risk: The words validated/certified were not justified mathematically.
action: Downgraded all such claims to conservative numerical screening.
status: Fixed by wording; rigorous interval proof remains open

risk: Same equations and implementation do not constitute independent replication.
action: Renamed it a perturbed-initialization sensitivity study.

finding: Exact ideal rules and rounded published operators were not separated early enough.
risk: Floating-point residuals could be mistaken for exact theorem hypotheses.
action: Introduced Q* versus Q-tilde and conditional-theorem wording.

finding: Hierarchy/path wording could imply connectedness or continuous dependence on k.
action: Stated that k indexes a discrete nested hierarchy only.

finding: Coverage percentage could be read probabilistically.
risk: Area depends on coordinate choice and test domain.
action: Defined it as rectangular coordinate-area fraction.

finding: Structural rules use relative minimax while mixed risk uses absolute error.
action: Added a remark distinguishing construction and selection objectives.
